text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient Name: Osei-Bonsu, Margaret
Date of Birth: [on file]
Medical Record Number: [on file]
Accession Number: SP-[on file]
Ordering Physician: Gynecologic Oncology Service
Procedure: Diagnostic Laparoscopy with Omental Biopsy
Clinical Indication: Bilateral adnexal masses, omental caking, ascites; rule out malignancy
Specimen Received: Fresh and formalin-fixed tissue

---

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:

  A. Omentum, biopsy ("omental cake")
  B. Peritoneal washing fluid, cytology (submitted concurrently; see cytology report addendum)

---

CLINICAL HISTORY:
47-year-old female presenting with a 4-month history of progressive abdominal distension, ascites, bilateral adnexal masses (right 7.2 cm, left 5.8 cm on CT), and omental caking. CA-125 markedly elevated at 3,847 U/mL. HE4: 412 pmol/L. Family history notable for maternal aunt with breast cancer at age 38 and maternal grandmother with ovarian cancer at age 55. Diagnostic laparoscopy performed to obtain tissue for histologic diagnosis and to assess surgical feasibility. Omental biopsy obtained laparoscopically.

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A: Received fresh and subsequently fixed in 10% neutral buffered formalin is an irregular fragment of fibroadipose tissue consistent with omentum, measuring 14.0 x 9.0 x 3.0 cm and weighing 187 grams. The outer surface is tan-white to gray and bosselated. On serial sectioning, the specimen is largely replaced by confluent gray-white, firm, rubbery tumor deposits with focal areas of chalky-white calcification and central necrosis. The normal lobular adipose architecture of the omentum is almost entirely effaced. No discrete cystic structures identified. Representative sections submitted in cassettes A1 through A7.

Specimen B: Received is 180 mL of red-tinged fluid labeled "peritoneal washing." The specimen is processed by cytocentrifugation and ThinPrep liquid-based cytology with Papanicolaou staining. Cell block prepared from the residual pellet. See cytology addendum.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A — Omentum, biopsy:
Sections show fibroadipose tissue of the omentum extensively infiltrated and largely replaced by malignant epithelial neoplasm. The tumor is arranged in sheets, irregular papillary clusters, slit-like glandular spaces, and solid nests. The neoplastic cells are large and polygonal with moderate to abundant pale eosinophilic cytoplasm. The nuclei are markedly enlarged, vesicular, and demonstrate severe pleomorphism with prominent, often cherry-red macronucleoli. Nuclear contours are irregular. Mitotic figures are numerous and include atypical forms (3-5 atypical mitoses per high-power field in the most active areas). Apoptotic bodies are conspicuous.

Psammoma bodies (laminated calcific concretions) are identified within the tumor nests and in areas of necrosis, a feature highly characteristic of serous carcinoma of Mullerian origin. Focal areas of geographic necrosis are present, consistent with high-grade biology. The tumor is associated with a desmoplastic stromal reaction composed of dense fibrous tissue with intermixed inflammatory infiltrate including scattered lymphocytes and plasma cells. Lymphovascular invasion is identified in at least one focus.

No mucinous differentiation is identified. No intracellular mucin by routine staining. No signet ring cells. No squamous differentiation. No clear cell morphology. No endometrioid glandular architecture. No evidence of low-grade serous carcinoma morphology (no rounded papillae with fibrovascular cores, no low nuclear grade). The overall morphologic appearance is that of a high-grade serous carcinoma (HGSC), FIGO grade 3.

---

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDIES:

A directed immunohistochemical panel was applied to formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded tissue sections from Specimen A. Results are as follows:

  WT-1 (Wilms Tumor-1):       POSITIVE — diffuse strong nuclear staining (3+) in >90% of tumor cells
  PAX8:                        POSITIVE — diffuse nuclear staining (3+) in >90% of tumor cells
  p53:                         ABERRANT — diffuse strong overexpression pattern ("block-type" positivity, 3+) in >95% of tumor cells; consistent with a TP53 gain-of-function missense mutation
  CK7 (cytokeratin 7):        POSITIVE — diffuse cytoplasmic staining (3+)
  CK20 (cytokeratin 20):      NEGATIVE — no staining in tumor cells
  CA-125 (MUC16):             POSITIVE — diffuse membranous/cytoplasmic staining (2-3+)
  ER (estrogen receptor):     WEAKLY POSITIVE — nuclear staining in approximately 30% of tumor cells (1+)
  PR (progesterone receptor): NEGATIVE
  Napsin-A:                   NEGATIVE — no staining
  CDX2:                       NEGATIVE — no staining
  SATB2:                      NEGATIVE — no staining
  HER2/neu (4B5):             2+ (equivocal; FISH for HER2 amplification: PENDING — see addendum)

  Ki-67 (MIB-1) proliferation index: Approximately 78% of tumor nuclei positive, confirming high-grade biology.

Interpretation of IHC panel: The combined expression of WT-1, PAX8, CK7, and CA-125, with negative CK20, negative napsin-A, and absent PR, along with aberrant p53 pattern, is consistent with high-grade serous carcinoma (HGSC) of Mullerian origin. Primary ovarian/fallopian tube/peritoneal origin is favored in the clinical context. Primary gastrointestinal, lung adenocarcinoma, and urothelial primaries are effectively excluded by this panel. Weakly positive ER (30%) is seen in a subset of HGSC and is not inconsistent with this diagnosis.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

  - HER2 FISH (fluorescence in situ hybridization): PENDING — specimen submitted to reference laboratory; results to be reported as addendum upon receipt
  - Germline genetic testing: Not performed on this specimen; patient referred for clinical germline testing through genetic counseling service
  - Molecular / NGS testing: Tissue banked for potential future molecular profiling per institutional protocol; not performed at this time

---

CYTOLOGY ADDENDUM — Specimen B (Peritoneal Washing):

The peritoneal washing cytology preparation demonstrates numerous clusters and three-dimensional papillary aggregates of atypical epithelial cells with features of malignancy. Individual malignant cells display enlarged, pleomorphic nuclei with irregular nuclear membranes, coarse chromatin, and prominent nucleoli. The cytoplasm is moderate and mildly vacuolated. Background shows blood, fibrin strands, scattered mesothelial cells, and inflammatory cells. Psammoma bodies are identified in multiple fields. The cell block sections confirm the presence of malignant epithelial cells.

Conclusion: POSITIVE FOR MALIGNANT CELLS. Morphologic features are consistent with high-grade serous carcinoma.

---

DIAGNOSIS:

A. OMENTUM, BIOPSY ("omental cake"):
   METASTATIC HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA
   — Diffuse omental involvement with geographic necrosis, psammoma bodies, and desmoplastic stroma
   — Lymphovascular invasion identified
   — Immunohistochemical profile: WT-1(+), PAX8(+), CK7(+), CA-125(+), p53 aberrant (diffuse strong overexpression, gain-of-function pattern), ER weakly positive (30%), CK20(-), napsin-A(-)
   — Ki-67 proliferation index: ~78%
   — Morphologic and immunohistochemical profile consistent with primary Mullerian (ovarian/fallopian tube) origin
   — HER2 FISH: PENDING (addendum to follow)

B. PERITONEAL WASHING, CYTOLOGY:
   POSITIVE FOR MALIGNANT CELLS
   — High-grade serous carcinoma, consistent with primary Mullerian origin

---

SYNOPTIC SUMMARY (CAP Protocol for Carcinoma of Unknown Primary, Peritoneum/Omentum):

  Procedure:                    Laparoscopic biopsy
  Specimen site:                Omentum
  Tumor configuration:          Diffuse infiltration (omental cake)
  Histologic type:              High-grade serous carcinoma
  Histologic grade:             FIGO Grade 3 (high grade)
  Mitotic rate:                 High (>10 mitoses/10 HPF)
  Necrosis:                     Present (geographic)
  Psammoma bodies:              Present
  Lymphovascular invasion:      Present
  Peritoneal washing cytology:  Positive for malignant cells
  Additional pathologic findings: Desmoplastic stroma; marked nuclear pleomorphism
  Ancillary studies:            See above

---

PATHOLOGIST'S NOTE:

This high-grade serous carcinoma involving the omentum, in a 47-year-old woman with bilateral adnexal masses, marked CA-125 elevation (3,847 U/mL), elevated HE4, positive peritoneal cytology, and a family history of breast and ovarian cancer, is strongly consistent with an advanced-stage primary ovarian or fallopian tube high-grade serous carcinoma with omental metastasis. The aberrant p53 immunostaining pattern (diffuse strong "block-type" overexpression in >95% of cells) is consistent with a TP53 gain-of-function mutation, as expected in the vast majority of HGSC. Correlation with clinical, radiologic, and operative findings is recommended for definitive primary site classification. Germline BRCA1/2 testing is strongly recommended given the clinical and family history, and has been initiated per the ordering clinician.

---

Signed:
Attending Pathologist: [Gynecologic Pathology Faculty, Department of Pathology]
Resident/Fellow: [Pathology Resident, PGY-3]
Dictated and Electronically Signed

Addendum Note: HER2 FISH result and any additional molecular studies will be appended to this report upon completion.
